narrative_summary:
# Discharge Summary Draft

## Executive Summary
John Doe (DOB 01/15/1970) was hospitalized from 03/01/2026 to 03/05/2026 for Principal diagnosis: Pneumonia. Patient treated with antibiotics and improved. At discharge the patient was in Patient discharged in stable condition. Follow-up plan: Primary care in 1 week. Patient should maintain scheduled outpatient care as documented.

## Patient Demographics
John Doe (DOB 01/15/1970).

## Admission Date
03/01/2026

## Discharge Date
03/05/2026

## Principal Diagnosis
Principal diagnosis: Pneumonia.

_Note: Apply formatting improvement for Principal Diagnosis_

## Secondary Diagnoses
Secondary diagnoses: Hypertension.

## Hospital Course
The patient was hospitalized and patient treated with antibiotics and improved.

_Note: Prefer clinician-style formatting for Hospital Course_

## Procedures
Procedures during this admission included Chest X-ray.

## Lab Results
MISSING – CLINICIAN REVIEW REQUIRED

## Discharge Medications
Discharge medication regimen:
- Aspirin 81mg by mouth (PO) daily

_Note: Apply formatting improvement for Discharge Medications_

## Allergies
Documented allergies: Penicillin.

## Follow-up Instructions
Recommended follow-up: Primary care in 1 week. Patient should maintain scheduled outpatient care as documented.

_Note: Prefer clinician-style formatting for Follow-up Instructions_

## Discharge Condition
Patient discharged in stable condition.

_Note: Prefer clinician-style formatting for Discharge Condition_

## Pending Results
Blood culture pending — PENDING

## Medication Changes
